content.full_text:
FPPC
FEB I 9 07 AH '90

METRIC PARTNERS
January 31, 1990


VIA FEDERAL EXPRESS

Ms. Katherine Donovan, General Counsel
California Fair Political
    Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 807
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE: New Form 721 Disclosure Requirements

Dear Ms. Donovan:

    On behalf of Metric Institutional Realty Advisors, L.P. ("MIRA"), I am requesting guidance from the Fair Political Practices Commission ("FPPC") as to the applicability of revised Government Code Section 87200. I spoke with Ms. Colleen McGee of the FPPC staff on January 30, 1990 about several questions we have regarding this matter and she suggested that I send you a list of our questions.

    MIRA is a California limited partnership and is registered as an investment adviser with the Securities and Exchange Commission under the Investment Advisers Act of 1940. Pursuant to applicable law, MIRA is required to provide annual disclosure statements to its clients and to keep detailed books and records which are available for examination by regulatory agencies. These books and records are also available for review by a client of MIRA with respect to the particular client's account activity.

    MIRA's business is limited to the management of real estate investment portfolios for institutions (e.g., corporate and government employees benefit plans). It is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Metric Partners, an Illinois general partnership, which provides real estate investments and services to the public.

    MIRA is presently negotiating an investment management agreement with the San Francisco City and County Employees' Retirement System ("SFERS") pursuant to which MIRA would acquire, manage and sell real property assets for SFERS.

    We note that recently-amended Government Code Section 87200 imposes FPPC Form 721 filing requirements on "public officials who manage public investments" and that the term "public officials" is defined in Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18700(a) to include "consultants" of a state or local government agency. Section 18700(a)(2) of the Regulations further defines the term

INVESTMENT REALTY
950 TOWER LANE P.O. BOX 5861 FOSTER CITY, CA 94404 (415) 378-7000

an affiliate of Metropolitan Life Insurance Company

California Fair Political Practices Commission
January 31, 1990
Page 2


"consultant".  However, neither the Form 721 nor the Regulations
appear to have been prepared with independent investment managers
in mind.  As a result, we seek clarification from the FPPC as to
a number of matters with respect to MIRA.

     (1)  MIRA's authority to acquire, manage and sell assets on
behalf of SFERS will be restricted by SFERS's "Written Guidelines"
which in essence may be amended at will by SFERS.  Is the
definition of "consultant" interpreted so as to distinguish between
non-discretionary and discretionary investment management services,
or to otherwise recognize some identifiable level of client
involvement in the investment decision-making process?  For
example, if a MIRA client were to reserve final authority as to
buy-hold-sell decisions with respect to real property, would this
mean MIRA would not be a "consultant" as to that client under the
Regulations?

     (2)  It is clear that the Regulations and Form 721 are
intended to cover "natural persons" and do not take into account
the wholly different character of a multi-employee investment
management organization.  Where an investment manager is an entity,
who should be the party that files Form 721?  Should the entity be
the filing party, or should an employee or employees file on an
individual basis?  It should be noted that no individual will have
any authority to "manage public investments"; rather, MIRA will
have this authority to the extent granted by the investment
management agreement.  MIRA and its affiliates employ a large
number of professionals, many of whom will be involved with the
SFERS account.  What if investment services are provided to an
account through the efforts of numerous natural persons within the
organization, each with varying levels and types of internal
responsibilities depending upon the particular service being
provided, which might be the initial investment of funds to acquire
properties, the day-to-day management of properties or the sale of
the properties?  What if committees are used to make decisions or
recommendations about the properties?  May one person be designated
as primary manager or client contact for sole filing purposes?

     (3)  Form 721 refers to the disclosure of investments held in
the "jurisdiction".

          (a)  On what basis are securities holdings considered to
be in [a] jurisdiction?  Are there tests such as the issuer's
principal place of business, percent of revenue or income from a
particular locale, number of local employees relative to world-wide
employment, or otherwise?  As a practical matter, how could a
reporting person determine if a large publicly-held corporation

California Fair Political Practices Commission
January 31, 1990
Page 3


based, say in New York, "does business" in a jurisdiction such as
San Francisco?  May an outside manager or employee avoid the
disclosure of holdings of securities of issuers whose securities
are listed for trading on a national securities exchange or on the
established NASDAQ over-the-counter market?

     (b)  May disclosure be limited to those assets which
would be subject to the "material financial effect" rules standards
in Regulation Section 18702?

     (4)  Does the filing obligation commence within (a) 10 days,
or (b) 30 days of (c) the "as of" date of a management agreement,
(d) its actual execution date or (e) the first date funds are
contributed to the manager?  Current rules do not take into account
the engagement of investment management organizations.

     (5)  Do you intend to promulgate new Regulations and a
separate form for the required filings of independent investment
managers or their employees?

     (6)  May investment managers with filing responsibility seek
and obtain confidential treatment for these filings?  Neither these
managers nor their employees are candidates for or holders of
public office, and no other category of private contractor to a
public agency is subject to the requirement of making a public
filing of personal financial condition.  Confidential treatment
would seem appropriate under these circumstances.

     Based on my conversation with Ms. McGee, I understand that
neither MIRA nor any of its personnel will be required to make a
filing under Section 87200 until, at the earliest, 30 days
following execution of the contemplated investment management
contract with SFERS.  We hope this will provide sufficient time for
the FPPC to respond to this letter.

     We appreciate your attention to these inquiries.  If you need
any further information concerning this request, please feel free
to contact the undersigned at (415) 378-7116.

                              Very truly yours,

                              /s/ Herman H. Howerton
                              Herman H. Howerton
                              Senior Vice President,
                              General Counsel

HHH/clr

sections.question_synthetic: Metric Partners/MIRA seeks FPPC guidance on the applicability of Government Code Section 87200 and Form 721 disclosure requirements to independent investment managers managing public investments, including clarification on consultant definitions, filing party identification, jurisdictional scope of investments, filing timeline commencement, and confidentiality treatment.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a private party to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No question/conclusion sections in FPPC format. Contains six numbered questions from the requestor. Document quality affected by fax/scan artifacts and unusual formatting. No evidence this is an FPPC advice letter response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Metric Partners/MIRA seeks FPPC guidance on the applicability of Government Code Section 87200 and Form 721 disclosure requirements to independent investment managers managing public investments, including clarification on consultant definitions, filing party identification, jurisdictional scope of investments, filing timeline commencement, and confidentiality treatment.

embedding.summary: Metric Partners, on behalf of MIRA (an investment adviser managing real estate portfolios), requests FPPC guidance regarding Form 721 disclosure requirements and Government Code Section 87200 applicability, raising six specific questions about consultant definitions, entity filing responsibilities, investment jurisdiction scope, filing timelines, and potential confidentiality treatment for investment manager filings.
